one_line_takeaway: GSPR trains an LLM guardrail via cold-start SFT plus GRPO with a flexible, taxonomy-as-variable prompt and rule-based format/safety/category rewards, beating RSafe by 45%+ in-domain category accuracy and generalizing better than baselines to four unseen-taxonomy out-of-domain benchmarks while producing the shortest reasoning traces.
key_finding: On in-domain testing (Table 2), GSPR w/ Cold-start reaches overall S-Acc 85.68/86.36 and C-Acc 78.32/77.89 for the Qwen2.5-7B/Qwen3-8B base models respectively, versus RSafe's overall S-Acc 84.22/84.00 and C-Acc only 30.17/21.71 -- the paper reports this as 'more than 45% overall C-Acc' improvement over RSafe. LlamaGuard3-8B and GuardReasoner-8B achieve near-0% C-Acc in-domain because they cannot follow the fine-grained category instruction at all. On out-of-domain testing (Table 3, four unseen-taxonomy datasets), GSPR w/ Cold-start again gets the best overall S-Acc (92.84-93.11) and C-Acc (77.89-79.85), described in the paper as a 6% S-Acc and 25% C-Acc average gain over the Qwen2.5 base model. C-Acc is consistently ~10 points below S-Acc across all models/datasets, and on HEx-PHI specifically the best C-Acc is only 58.28% even though every model exceeds 95% S-Acc, indicating category-level generalization is markedly harder than binary safety classification. Response-efficiency analysis (Table 4) shows GSPR w/ Cold-start produces the fewest average words per response among explanation-giving models (34.10 words for Qwen2.5-based, 77.73 for Qwen3-based) with near-zero language-mixing (Mix% 0.06/0.04) and low repetition, versus RSafe (187.98/362.46 words) and Gemini-2.5-Flash/o3-mini (239/173 words).
core_contribution: GSPR (Generalizable Safety Policy Reasoner) is an LLM-based content-moderation guardrail trained with a flexible prompt template that treats the safety taxonomy itself as a variable, rather than a fixed set of hard-coded risk categories baked into the prompt. It is trained in two stages: (1) supervised cold-start fine-tuning on 1,383 samples of per-policy safety reasoning traces distilled from Gemini-2.5-Flash (80 samples per taxonomy across 19 taxonomies, filtered by regex), followed by (2) Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) with a rule-based composite reward: a format reward (correct \safety{}/\category{} tag order, single-language output, n-gram repetition penalty, and a length penalty for verbose post-think generations), a binary safety-label reward, and a binary fine-grained category-label reward, combined as R_i = R_f_i x (alpha1*R_s_i + alpha2*R_c_i). Because the taxonomy is sampled into the prompt at train time (with an 'others' catch-all category), the resulting model generalizes to unseen safety policies at inference rather than being locked to one benchmark's fixed label set, unlike prior guardrails (Llama Guard, ShieldGemma, WildGuard, RSafe, GuardReasoner) which the paper's own Table 1 shows use fixed taxonomies.
threat_model: The paper addresses LLM content-moderation/guardrail deployment, not an adversarial attack technique. The guardrail model G moderates both (a) a user's input prompt and (b) a target LLM's prompt-response pair, classifying each as safe/unsafe and, if unsafe, assigning the most relevant fine-grained risk category from a supplied policy list. The paper frames upstream risks abstractly (jailbreaking, red-teaming, prompt injection, RAG-corrupted outputs) only as motivation in the introduction -- these are not evaluated or attacked in the paper; the actual empirical work is classification accuracy of the guardrail on existing labeled safety benchmarks, not adversarial robustness testing against attacks.
limitations: The paper itself does not evaluate GSPR against adversarial jailbreak or prompt-injection attacks -- the abstract's threat framing (jailbreaking, red-teaming, RAG corruption) is motivational context only, not a tested attack surface; robustness against adaptive attackers is out of scope. Cold-start distillation depends on Gemini-2.5-Flash-generated reasoning traces filtered by regex, which could propagate distillation artifacts or biases from that teacher model into the guardrail. The RSafe baseline is a reimplementation by the authors (no official weights exist), so comparisons to RSafe carry some reproduction uncertainty acknowledged implicitly by the authors' need to reimplement it. Category-level generalization remains substantially weaker than safety-level generalization even for GSPR (e.g., 58.28% best C-Acc on HEx-PHI), which the authors flag as an open gap. The paper states future work will add more safety benchmarks to further improve generalization, implicitly acknowledging the current 19-taxonomy/167-policy training coverage is not exhaustive.

datasets_used: GUARDSET-X (training, incl. HR policies of 11 tech companies and education domain)
quantitative_results: In-domain overall S-Acc: GSPR w/ Cold-start 85.68 (Qwen2.5) / 86.36 (Qwen3) vs RSafe 84.22 / 84.00 vs Gemini-2.5-Flash 73.02 vs o3-mini 74.81
In-domain overall C-Acc: GSPR w/ Cold-start 78.32 (Qwen2.5) / 77.89 (Qwen3) vs RSafe 30.17 / 21.71 (paper reports >45% overall C-Acc gain over RSafe)
Out-of-domain overall S-Acc: GSPR w/ Cold-start 92.84 (Qwen2.5) / 93.11 (Qwen3); overall C-Acc 79.7 / 79.85; paper reports ~6% S-Acc and ~25% C-Acc average gain over Qwen2.5 base model
HEx-PHI out-of-domain: best C-Acc only 58.28% (GSPR w/ Cold-start, Qwen2.5) while all models exceed 95% S-Acc
Response length (Avg Word #, in-domain): GSPR w/ Cold-start 34.10 words (Qwen2.5-based) / 77.73 (Qwen3-based) vs RSafe 187.98 / 362.46 vs Gemini-2.5-Flash 239.06 vs o3-mini 172.89
Language mixing (Mix%, in-domain): GSPR w/ Cold-start 0.06% (Qwen2.5) / 0.04% (Qwen3) vs RSafe 0.68% (Qwen2.5) / 25.23% (Qwen3)
Ablation: 'GSPR (safety only)' (flexible prompt, no category reward) reaches 66% C-Acc in-domain and 72% C-Acc out-of-domain, already far above RSafe, isolating the prompt-template contribution

Ablation: adding category reward + cold-start ('GSPR w/o Cold-start' -> 'GSPR w/ Cold-start') raises overall category accuracy to ~78%; cold-start alone brings >20% C-Acc gain under Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct
Training cost: ~40 GPU-days on 8x NVIDIA H800
Cold-start data: 1,383 filtered samples distilled from Gemini-2.5-Flash across 19 taxonomies, 80 sampled points per taxonomy before filtering
baselines_compared: Gemini-2.5-Flash (closed-source CoT API, also used as cold-start teacher)
RSafe (RL-aligned guardrail, reimplemented by authors due to no public weights, trained on same data as GSPR)
